statement: Provide visible focus rings using :focus-visible, grouped with :focus-within where appropriate.
rationale: Keyboard users must see where focus is.

statement: Never set outline: none without providing a visible focus replacement.
Support full keyboard interaction per the WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices.
Use hit targets of at least 24px (44px on mobile); expand the hit area if the visual is smaller.
Set touch-action: manipulation to prevent double-tap zoom delays.
Use a font-size of at least 16px on mobile inputs to prevent iOS zoom.
Loading buttons show a spinner and keep their original label.
rationale: Avoids layout shift and keeps the action legible while pending.

statement: Never block paste in inputs or textareas.
Enter submits a focused single-line input; in a textarea, Cmd/Ctrl+Enter submits.
Show validation errors inline next to fields and focus the first error on submit.
Set autocomplete and a meaningful name, with the correct type and inputmode.
Warn before navigating away from unsaved changes.
Use <a>/<Link> for navigation so Cmd/Ctrl/middle-click work.
Never use a div with onClick for navigation.
Reflect state in the URL (filters, tabs, pagination, expanded panels) for deep-linking.
Confirm destructive actions or provide an Undo window.
Announce toasts and inline validation with a polite aria-live region.
Use optimistic UI; reconcile on response and roll back or offer Undo on failure.
Honor prefers-reduced-motion with a reduced or disabled variant.
Animate only compositor-friendly properties (transform, opacity).
Never use transition: all; list the properties explicitly.
Verify layouts on mobile, laptop, and ultra-wide (simulate ultra-wide at 50% zoom).
Respect safe areas using env(safe-area-inset-*).
Give flex children min-width: 0 so they can truncate.
Prefer native semantics (button, a, label, table) before ARIA.
Give icon-only buttons a descriptive aria-label.
Convey status with more than color alone; pair icons with text labels.
Use font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums when comparing numbers in columns.
Set explicit image dimensions to prevent layout shift (CLS).
Virtualize large lists (more than about 50 items).
Preload above-the-fold images and lazy-load the rest.
Never disable browser zoom (user-scalable=no or maximum-scale=1).
Set color-scheme: dark on <html> for dark themes.
Give native <select> an explicit background-color and color (Windows fix).
On dark or saturated brand-color (e.g. corporate red) backgrounds, use the all-white monochrome logo — the corner mark and every letter white.
rationale: The two-color logo loses the grey "IN" and puts red on red.

statement: Never place the two-color (red/grey) logo on a dark or red background; switch to the all-white logo instead.

The corner mark may stand alone as a decorative marker signalling TEBIN authorship — typically the top-right corner of a photo or slide. Keep it brand red on light backgrounds and white on dark or red ones.
